Entity_Description: Campbell River is a city on the east coast of Vancouver Island, British Columbia, at the south end of Discovery Passage opposite Quadra Island. As of the 2021 Census its population was 35,519. Long branded the "Salmon Capital of the World," the community grew from a forestry and sport-fishing base and now leans on aquaculture, tourism, health care and the trades. It anchors the Strathcona Regional District, serves as the gateway to Strathcona Provincial Park, and sits below Elk Falls and BC Hydro's John Hart generating station on the Campbell River.

As a local {BUSINESS_TYPE}, we know the wet coastal climate and the mix of mill-era houses and newer subdivisions here. {ESTIMATE_TYPE} with {RESPONSE_TIME}. Call {PHONE}.
Short_Description: Known worldwide as the "Salmon Capital of the World," Campbell River sits where Discovery Passage funnels tidal water between Vancouver Island and Quadra Island, a setting that has drawn anglers to the Tyee Pool and Discovery Pier for generations. That same saltwater exposure - wind-driven rain off the Strait of Georgia, salt-laden air, and a housing stock running from Campbellton's mill-era homes to newer Willow Point subdivisions - shapes what local property owners need from {SERVICE}. {COMPANY_NAME} works across the city and its outlying areas, bringing {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} to every job. Whether you own a view home above Discovery Passage or a rancher near the highway, our team delivers {SERVICE} built for this coast. {CTA_PRIMARY} - call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Campbell River grew up around resource work and the water, and both still define the place. The city stretches along the shoreline of Discovery Passage, the tidal channel that separates Vancouver Island from Quadra Island and gave the town its reputation among salmon anglers - the Tyee Pool at the river mouth and the community-built Discovery Pier are still landmarks locals name without thinking. North of downtown, Campbellton carries the older mill-town housing stock; south along the highway, Willow Point and the newer subdivisions climb the slope with ocean-view homes and 1970s-to-modern ranchers. Each of these building types asks something different from {SERVICE}, and a local {BUSINESS_TYPE} has to know the difference.

The coastal climate here is the constant. Rain sweeps in off the Strait of Georgia, salt air rides the wind up Discovery Passage, and winters stay mild but wet - conditions that wear on exteriors, drainage, and anything left exposed to the weather. {COMPANY_NAME} plans {SERVICE} around that reality, not around a generic checklist. {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION}

Campbell River's economy shapes the work too. The old Elk Falls pulp-and-paper era has given way to aquaculture, tourism, health care and the trades, and the city serves as the supply-and-service hub for the Discovery Islands, Strathcona Provincial Park - British Columbia's oldest - and the logging and fish-farm operations up the coast. That means we handle everything from downtown commercial {PROPERTY_TYPE} to remote-feeling waterfront properties and acreages out toward Black Creek and the Oyster River.

Just upstream, Elk Falls and BC Hydro's John Hart generating station sit on the Campbell River, a reminder of how tied this community is to water and power. Our crews travel the whole footprint - the core neighbourhoods, the Quadra Island ferry corridor, and outlying areas like Courtenay to the south - so coverage is never a question. When you call {COMPANY_NAME} for {SERVICE}, you get a team that treats Campbell River as home ground, offers {ESTIMATE_TYPE}, backs the work with a {RATING} reputation, and answers with {RESPONSE_TIME}. {TAGLINE} Reach us at {PHONE} or {EMAIL}.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 35,519 (2021 Census)
- Located on the east coast of Vancouver Island along Discovery Passage, opposite Quadra Island
- Long known as the "Salmon Capital of the World," home to the historic Tyee Pool and the Discovery Pier saltwater fishing pier
- Anchors the Strathcona Regional District and is the gateway to Strathcona Provincial Park, British Columbia's oldest provincial park
- Elk Falls and BC Hydro's John Hart Generating Station sit on the Campbell River just above the city
- Economy has shifted from forestry (the former Elk Falls pulp mill) toward aquaculture, tourism, health care and the trades
- About 174 km from Vancouver; nearby areas include Quadra Island (~19 km), Cortes Island (~23 km) and Courtenay (~41 km)
